cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: Does not run due to issued related to Uplay. A workaround is to use a crack to bypass Uplay (disables online features), the game runs but crashes after the first cutscene.
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Sound doesnt work ingame. Otherwise it runs quite well. Suggested workaround to run as winXP doesnt seem to work. Could be it only works with wine-staging for some reason.
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Still looses sound after 1st cutscene - but other then that runs quite smooth
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Didn't have time to test extensively, but everything appears to be running okay. Had to use protontricks to install xact and winecfg to set FarCry4.exe to Windows XP to get sound working. Also using a Corsair mouse causes the game to freeze for some reason. A cheap wireless mouse did not exhibit this issue.
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Runs out of the box, 70-90 fps. No audio after menu, but can be fixed installing xact installed as described here: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/2143#issuecomment-471496701
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Sound wasn't working. So I installed protontweaks and used it to add xact, and all of the x3daudio and xaudio libs in winecfg.
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Needed xact from protontricks, also 1.9-2 version of cabextract for debian (sid). Also set to compatibility mode Windows XP. Finally, moving the mouse was making the game hang so disabled Uplay overlay and voila! Works perfectly!
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Game runs well, however menu is very slow and in addition, sound does not work post first cutscene. I have tried adding xact via protontricks (on 0bs1d1an overlay), and added xaudio2_7 with winecfg, but it still continues to not play audio post first cutscene. Thus, while it is able to run, it is with issue on the audio (and doesn't even display subtitles post cut scene).
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Update to my last report: It is necessary to add WinXP compatibility through the winecfg menu (WINEPREFIX="<STEAM DIR>/steamapps/compatdata/298110/pfx" winecfg), which will fix sound, however the game now crashes in either second cutscene where they are having a meal, or post second cut scene where you are searching around. I disabled d3d11 and enabled wine d3d11 via the launch options with: "PROTON_VARIABLE=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11 PROTON_VARIABLE=1 PROTON_USE_WINED3D", however crashing persists.
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Installed xact using Protontricks, change Windows version to XP, and turned off UPlay in-game overlay results in a game just as playable as it is on Windows.
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Tweaks: xact and winXP to get sound working.
title: Far Cry® 4

notes: Added xact via Protontricks and changed compatability mode to Windows XP in winecfg but still no sound until all of the x3daudio and xaudio libs were added in winecfg. Had to disable the Uplay overlay within Uplay, no option to disable it while in-game. FPS was mostly between 50-60 on High but dipped to around 40 on some occasions. Crashed 3 times in 37 hours of playtime.
title: Far Cry® 4
